content: Which one of the following statements best describes the acidic/basic/amphoteric nature of ZnO and CaO ?

options.content: ZnO is amphoteric, while CaO is basic.
ZnO is basic, while CaO is amphoteric.
ZnO is acidic, while CaO is basic.

explanation:
• ZnO (Zinc oxide):

    It can react with both acids and bases.

    For example:

  • With acid: $ \text{ZnO} + 2HCl \rightarrow ZnCl_2 + H_2O $

  • With base: $ \text{ZnO} + 2NaOH + H_2O \rightarrow Na_2[Zn(OH)_4] $

    → Hence, ZnO is amphoteric.

  • CaO (Calcium oxide):

    It reacts with water to form Ca(OH)_2, a strong base, and reacts with acids to form salts, but not with bases.

    → Hence, CaO is basic.

✅ Correct answer: Option A

ZnO is amphoteric, while CaO is basic.

content: $$ \text { What are the correct orders of stability for the following compounds? } $$
explanation:
1️⃣ Comparison of $ \mathrm{VF_5}$ and $ \mathrm{VCl_5}$

  • Vanadium pentahalides involve the +5 oxidation state of vanadium ($V^{5+}$).

  • As we move from F to I, the +5 oxidation state becomes less stable because:

  • Larger halides ($Cl^-$, $Br^-$, $I^-$) are less electronegative and less able to stabilize a high positive oxidation state on the metal.

  • Fluorine is the most electronegative and forms stronger, more covalent, and more stable bonds with highly oxidized metals.

✅ Thus,

$ \mathrm{VF_5 > VCl_5} \text{ (in stability)} $

2️⃣ Comparison of $ \mathrm{CuCl_2}$ and $ \mathrm{CuI_2}$

  • In Cu(+2) compounds, we must consider redox tendencies:

  • $ \mathrm{Cu^{2+} + I^- → Cu^+ + \frac{1}{2} I_2} $

  • The above reaction indicates that $ \mathrm{CuI_2} $ is unstable; it disproportionates to $ \mathrm{CuI} $ (Cu⁺) and $ \mathrm{I_2} $.

  • $ \mathrm{CuCl_2} $ is stable because $ \mathrm{Cl^-} $ is not easily oxidized, hence no such disproportionation.

✅ So,

$ \mathrm{CuCl_2 > CuI_2} \text{ (in stability)} $

✅ Final conclusion

$ \boxed{\mathrm{VF_5 > VCl_5; \, CuCl_2 > CuI_2}} $

Correct option: A
